PrivateDAO Workflow Review
Community-native intake
Review -> Approve -> Execute -> Proof
PrivateDAO execution layer
PrivateDAO is the operating system for everything that happens after governance

DAO communities do not need to abandon Telegram or Discord on day one. They need a private, verifiable path for what happens after a proposal passes: review, approval, treasury execution, and audit proof.

ConnectReviewSignVerify
Open governOpen executeOpen proof
Create PrivateDAO Workflow

Proposal passes. The real operational pain starts next.

Reviewers move to chats, founders chase approvals in DMs, budgets drift into spreadsheets, and the final treasury action becomes hard to explain. PrivateDAO keeps the community conversation where it is, then turns the sensitive execution path into a private and verifiable workflow.

Start PrivateDAO workflowOpen live prepare APIVerify proofOpen judge path
Workflow spine
1Paste PrivateDAO proposal link
2Create coordination room
3Invite reviewers
4Record encrypted reviews
5Approve treasury request
6Execute payout
7Publish audit proof
Without PrivateDAO

Governance ends. Coordination fragments.

Telegram and Discord are good for conversation, but they are not enough for private review, treasury approval, execution lineage, or audit proof. That gap is where teams lose trust after a proposal passes.

01Proposal Passed
02Telegram
03DMs
04Spreadsheets
05Chaos
With PrivateDAO

The post-governance path becomes executable.

PrivateDAO does not replace community tools. It wraps them with reviewer assignment, encrypted reviews, approval proofs, treasury execution, and audit receipts that can be tested from a wallet on Solana Testnet.

01Proposal Passed
02Review
03Approve
04Execute
05Audit
Brand explainer

The simple story: governance passes, then PrivateDAO runs the work.

This short brand video is separate from the live demo. It explains why teams keep Telegram and Discord for conversation, then move review, approval, execution, and audit into a wallet-first Solana Testnet flow.

Multisig
Squads v4, 2-of-3 threshold
Timelock
48 hours before protected execution

Coordination sources

Do not replace community tools. Bind them to proof.

SrMessi's point is the adoption path: users should keep Telegram, Discord, GitHub, Notion, and forum discussion. PrivateDAO becomes the layer for reviewer assignment, encrypted review, treasury approval, execution receipt, and audit proof.

Telegram
@privateDAOOS
Keep the first conversation where the community already lives.
Discord
PrivateDAO Discord
Bring reviewers and contributors into a shared coordination channel.
Notion
Paste review brief
Attach the working brief without turning it into the source of truth.
GitHub Issue
Paste issue link
Keep public implementation discussion while review scores stay private.
Forum Thread
Paste proposal thread
Anchor the governance source before moving into approval and proof.
PrivateDAO layer
Reviewer Assignment

Map reviewers to a passed proposal without exposing every reviewer's full reasoning publicly.

Encrypted Reviews

Keep scores, notes, conflicts, and rationale private until the correct disclosure step.

Approval Proofs

Generate a wallet-visible proof path before treasury execution.

Treasury Request

Move from approved review into governed payout, receipt, and audit continuity.

On-chain claim console

Anchor any privacy rail as a visitor-signed Testnet claim

This console makes every privacy and encryption lane end-to-end testable from the browser. The visitor selects the rail, encrypts the claim locally, signs a Solana Testnet commitment transaction, then verifies the signature on-chain. Stronger rails still keep their native REFHE, MagicBlock, Ika, Umbra, Jupiter, and Torque proof paths beside this universal claim layer.

Repeatable means every visitor can run a fresh live attempt. Each click creates a new AES-GCM encrypted claim packet and a new wallet-signed Testnet commitment from that visitor wallet, not a replay of an old project signature.

Confidential coordination graph
Market Decision→Grant Review→Treasury Approval→Payroll Allocation→Execution→Prove→Audit Proof
01
Review rail
02
Encrypt locally
03
Sign digest
04
Prove actors
05
Verify receipt
Get Testnet SOLClaims JSONPrepare on-chain memoMatrix JSON
PrivateDAO grant review workflow
tier 1

PrivateDAO-native grant review workflow that turns a market-passed decision into confidential review, treasury approval, payout, prove layer, and public audit receipt.

A market decision passes, grant reviewers are assigned, a private scoring room opens, treasury approves the award, payout executes, and the public receives an audit receipt.

Market passesAssign reviewersPrivate scoringTreasury approvalGrant payoutProveAudit receipt
Role-based coordination
ContributorReviewerTreasury ManagerAuditor
Workflow graph
Market Decision→Grant Review→Treasury Approval→Payroll Allocation→Execution→Prove→Audit Proof
Prove layer
Prove: who reviewed
Prove: who approved
Prove: who executed
Prove: which receipt proves outcome
Private: Reviewer notes, private scores, applicant context, and treasury reasoning stay encrypted before final award disclosure.
Audit: Market state, reviewer quorum digest, treasury approval digest, payout reference, and final audit receipt become verifiable.
claim: visitor-wallet-memo-attestation
native: metadao-market-to-grant-workflow-plus-visitor-wallet-memo-attestation
Open service
Connect a Testnet wallet, pick a privacy rail, and anchor the claim on-chain.
Selective disclosure receipt
After anchoring, verify the encrypted packet locally, export a public attestation without the AES key, or export a private disclosure receipt for an allowed reviewer.
PrivateDAO is confidential coordination infrastructure for organizations on Solana, connecting private operations to verifiable outcomes.
Developed in public through github.com/X-PACT/PrivateDAO with product proof, runtime evidence, and final security hardening moving in parallel.

© PrivateDAO. Core source remains open where the repository license applies. Brand, product design, documentation, digital evidence, and official deployments are reserved; official-looking derivative deployments, service packaging, or commercial use require written coordination through official PrivateDAO channels.
